title: Spotify and labels win (notional) $322m from Anna’s Archive - Music Ally
text:
We say ‘notional’ because there is surely very little chance of getting this money. Nonetheless, Spotify and the three major labels have won a default judgement against Anna’s Archive.
That’s the site that
announced before Christmas
that it had scraped metadata and recordings from Spotify, and was planning to make them publicly available.
Billboard reported on the judgement
, which came after Anna’s Archive’s still-unknown owners failed to respond to the lawsuit. Spotify has been awarded $300m in damages as part of the ruling, with the three majors awarded $22.2m collectively.
Enforcing these damages will be challenging, to say the least, although the report noted that the ruling also requires ISPs to block Anna’s Archive – which since December has been hopping domains to stay open under pressure from the plaintiffs.
Spotify and the three majors
launched their lawsuit in January
, then
sought a default judgement in March
due to the lack of response. The ‘
AnnaArchivist
‘ profile on Reddit, which appeared to be the official voice of the site’s owners, hasn’t posted in 16 days at the time of writing.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
